title: CatBoost with native categoricals and ordered statistics on interaction keys
summary: The dataset has high-cardinality entities (`Driver`, `Race`, `Race_Year`) and likely strong interactions.
rationale: The dataset has high-cardinality entities (`Driver`, `Race`, `Race_Year`) and likely strong interactions. CatBoost’s ordered categorical processing is specifically designed to reduce target leakage versus manual mean encoding or crude frequency encoding.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoostClassifier using native categorical columns plus interaction strings like `Driver|Compound`, `Race|Compound`, `Driver|Race`, `Year|Race|Compound`. Compare `boosting_type=Ordered` and `Plain`, but score only with `GroupKFold` on `Race_Year`.
expected_effect: Can capture sparse category interactions that the current numeric feature stack likely underuses; often complementary to LightGBM/XGBoost in tabular competitions.
risk: If fold construction is weak, category priors will look better locally than on leaderboard. Ordered boosting may also be slower.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
